description_long: This object is a semi-automatic pistol-style firearm with a long, slender barrel and a distinctive toggle-lock mechanism on top, reminiscent of the iconic Luger P08. The frame and barrel are finished in a matte gray, while the grip panels are a dark brown, likely simulating wood or a composite material. The trigger guard is rounded and the trigger itself is visible within, with a prominent circular ring at the rear of the frame. The barrel is cylindrical and extends significantly forward from the main body, giving the weapon a unique profile. The top of the receiver features a raised toggle assembly, and the overall design is compact yet elongated, suitable for one-handed operation. The magazine is not externally visible, suggesting an internal or grip-inserted design.
description: A classic pistol-style firearm with a long barrel, toggle-lock mechanism, and dark brown grip panels. The frame is matte gray, and the design is compact yet elongated.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, toggle-lock mechanism, and dark brown grip panel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the symmetrical alignment of the upper receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the grip, trigger guard, and toggle-lock mechanism from the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the grip and the toggle assembly, with the grip's curvature visible.
